meaning: vi.偷懒,混日子vt.消磨n.懒散,闲散
examples: I was happy enough to laze about on the beach.
He went to Spain for nine months, to laze around and visit relations.
Laze on the white-sand beach or golf on two world-ranked courses.
Old timers laze away the hot days playing with grandkids and tending to tasks like drying fish and preserving salted duck egg yolks in the sun.
